title: Process, Physical Distribution, and Logistics Consulting Services

narrative.executive_view: Logistics consulting offers meaningful AI leverage in analysis, documentation and back-office work, but the investment case depends on commercial structure. Gainshare, proprietary benchmark data and implementation-heavy engagements can retain savings; generic project analysis faces fee compression and client bypass through AI-enabled supply-chain software.
narrative.how_ai_changes_work: Network modeling, inventory analysis, freight-rate review, forecasting, report drafting and clerical audit work are increasingly machine-assisted. Site observation, facility implementation, stakeholder interviews, operational change and accountability during WMS or TMS deployment remain human-intensive. The role estimates therefore automate task bundles rather than whole engagements.
narrative.value_capture: Consulting supports time-and-materials, fixed-fee and outcome/value-based structures [S3]. AI savings flow most directly to an operator under gainshare or a well-scoped fixed fee; hours-based work and competitively rebid studies pass savings to clients. Proprietary data and recurring platform-enabled delivery can improve retention, but Equiteq's buyer evidence shows those features must be demonstrated rather than presumed [S4].
narrative.adoption_timing: The 2025 MHI-Deloitte survey reports AI in use at 28% of responding supply-chain organizations and a five-year expectation of 82% [S1]. That is client-side rather than consultant-specific evidence, but it indicates adoption below the 50% boundary today and a plausible crossing within a few years. CSCMP's 2026 report confirms that core-workflow use remains uneven [S2].
narrative.consolidation_economics: The deterministic target pool is real but heterogeneous, and it unusually includes some 500-plus-employee, low-margin firms. Equiteq describes sustained buyer conviction for specialized, data-enabled and platform-like consultancies, while broad generalists face pressure [S4]. Entry and exit multiples are therefore low-confidence estimates conditional on owner independence, vertical depth and recurring or gainshare economics.
narrative.terminal_demand: Terminal demand is PRESSURED rather than melting. Physical warehouse implementation, field diagnosis, change management and executive accountability remain paid services, especially when disruption makes execution urgent. Pure network-design, benchmarking and forecast studies are more vulnerable because clients can access similar analysis through their own software and AI-equipped teams.
narrative.risks_and_uncertainty: No source isolates US NAICS 541614 by fee model, owner age, adoption or transaction multiple. Supply-chain surveys represent buyers and operators, professional-services pricing evidence spans many consulting types, and M&A evidence covers specialty consulting broadly. The four-digit occupational proxy also likely understates logisticians, industrial engineers and field personnel, so implementation-heavy targets may be less automatable than the central case.

sources.title: MHI and Deloitte 2025 Annual Industry Report release
sources.what_it_supports: Client-side supply-chain AI adoption: 28% in use and 82% expected within five years, plus use cases and data constraints.

sources.title: CSCMP State of Logistics Report
sources.what_it_supports: Current logistics context showing uneven AI adoption, isolated point solutions and measured returns in specific applications.

sources.title: Deltek consulting pricing models guide
sources.what_it_supports: Use of time-and-materials, fixed, retainer and value/outcome-based consulting fee structures and their margin implications.

sources.title: Equiteq Specialty Consulting M&A Report — 2H 2025
sources.what_it_supports: Selective consulting M&A, buyer preference for specialization, proprietary data, platforms, recurring models and demonstrable AI execution.

sources.title: BizBuySell 2025 business valuation guide
sources.what_it_supports: Micro-business transaction context: 2025 service-business average earnings multiple of 2.72x on discretionary earnings, with explicit unit and size mismatch.

ai_replaceable_share.rationale: Supply-chain AI use cases directly affect analysis, prediction, recommendation and documentation, while physical implementation and organizational change remain outside the readily automated task set.
ai_replaceable_share.plausible_range: 0.29-0.43 of total wage bill after role weighting
caveats: Sources measure client-side supply-chain adoption and applications, not task shares inside NAICS 541614 consultancies.
The supplied role mix is four-digit 5416 and likely understates logisticians, industrial engineers and field implementation staff.
Adoption pace, fee capture and terminal demand are not used to inflate these task fractions.

role_judgments.rationale: Modeling, benchmarking and report production compress; interviews, site diagnosis and change leadership persist.
Internal reporting automates, while principal-led selling and executive accountability persist.
Market scans and content production automate, but positioning and buyer interpretation remain.
Proposal preparation compresses; relationship selling to operations executives remains.
Schedules and status work automate; cross-site implementation coordination persists.
Routine inquiries, scheduling and document handling are highly exposed, with escalations remaining.
Operational analysis and documentation are exposed, while client-specific field judgment persists.
Configuration, analytics code and testing are assisted; integration ownership remains.
Freight-audit, reconciliation and invoicing tasks automate substantially; exception judgment remains.
Recruiting administration and documents automate, while people judgment persists.
The large tail mixes exposed analysts and back office with less-exposed logisticians, engineers and field staff.

pi_dist.rationale: Value/outcome and gainshare-like structures can retain efficiency, while time-and-materials and effort-benchmarked project fees pass it through; specialty buyers reward firms that have already built repeatable models.
caveats: No source measures the fee-model mix for the target EBITDA band or this NAICS code.
Gainshare prevalence in freight audit is not quantified here and therefore receives no direct observed weight.

pi_moat.rationale: Operational context, implementation accountability and proprietary benchmarks create moderate retention, but client-side platforms can internalize analysis and generic advice is readily rebid.
caveats: Equiteq describes preferred consulting attributes broadly rather than measuring logistics-consulting switching costs.
Physical implementation supports terminal demand separately and is not itself counted as capture.

t50_years.rationale: Current client-side use is 28%, with 82% expected within five years; an approximately two-to-three-year crossing is a bounded interpolation moderated by CSCMP's evidence of uneven core-workflow use.
caveats: Client-side adoption is a proxy for consultant delivery adoption.
The 82% figure is a five-year expectation, not an observed diffusion curve.

current_adoption_pct.rationale: The MHI-Deloitte 2025 release directly reports 28% of surveyed supply-chain leaders have AI in use today.
caveats: This is observed for surveyed supply-chain organizations, not US lower-middle-market 541614 consulting firms.
Use of any AI is broader than effective adoption across consulting delivery tasks.

historical_analogs.value: Supply-chain digitization moved analysis into optimization, planning and visibility tools while preserving integration and change-management work; generative and agentic AI deepen that same software-embedding pattern.
historical_analogs.rationale: Both sources frame AI within a continuing sequence of supply-chain technology, automation and orchestration investments.
historical_analogs.plausible_range: Incremental analytical automation to substantial software-led client bypass
caveats: This qualitative analog does not set role fractions or adoption timing by itself.

selection.rationale: No fresh owner-age statistic specific to US logistics consultancies was found, so a conservative unmeasured estimate is used.
caveats: This is not evidence of a sector succession wave.

succession_shortage_documented.rationale: No 541614-specific succession shortage was documented in the fresh research pass.
caveats: Individual firms may still have material succession risk.

active_consolidators.rationale: A bounded buyer count reflects strategic and PE platforms actively pursuing differentiated specialty consultancies, discounted because logistics consulting is not separately enumerated.
caveats: This is not a direct count of bidders for 541614 firms.
Used only for buyability, not adoption or task exposure.

buy_mult.rationale: A six-times EBITDA entry sits above the micro-market SDE context but discounts generic consulting relative to differentiated specialty assets; it is a normalized target-band estimate, not a sourced transaction median.
caveats: BizBuySell's 2.72x service-business figure is discretionary earnings for much smaller firms, not EBITDA for the target band.
Partner compensation, earnouts and cyclicality require normalization.

exit_mult.rationale: The exit assumes a shift toward demonstrably specialized, data-enabled and repeatable delivery—the qualities Equiteq says buyers reward—without assuming a platform-level premium.
caveats: No direct 541614 transaction multiple supports the selected expansion.
Exit uplift is conditional on reduced founder dependence and stronger recurring or gainshare revenue.

pricing_structure.value: Mixed time-and-materials, fixed-project, retainer and value/outcome pricing; freight savings work may use gainshare, but its share is not measured.
pricing_structure.rationale: Deltek documents the broad consulting fee menu; the selected description preserves uncertainty about the code-specific mix.
pricing_structure.plausible_range: Effort-dominated project book to outcome/gainshare-heavy specialist
caveats: No direct evidence quantifies gainshare, fixed-fee or T&M revenue in NAICS 541614.

cross_checks.uplift_vs_A_consistent: Role-weighted task exposure is independent of diffusion. Current adoption uses client-side survey evidence and remains below 50%, so t50 is positive; neither target count nor deal activity affects V or A.

terminal_value.justification: Physical implementation, operational change and proprietary context preserve paid demand, but software-native modeling and AI-enabled internal teams can replace generic analytical advisory. The surviving share depends on actual implementation revenue.

cross_checks.no_proxy_confirmed: Every external source is treated as a broader supply-chain, consulting-pricing, specialty-M&A or micro-transaction proxy; none is labeled a direct 541614 lower-middle-market measurement.
cross_checks.pricing_model_deflation: Effort-based and rebid project fees transmit reduced hours to clients, while value/gainshare structures retain more savings. This channel is modeled in pi_dist and not counted again in technical task exposure.

dataset_mismatch.description: Labor share combines 2024 wages with 2022 receipts, and the occupation mix is four-digit 5416 rather than six-digit 541614. The 2022 receipts base also predates current agentic supply-chain tools.
sensitivity: More field engineers and logisticians would lower replaceable task share.
A post-freight-cycle revenue base could change labor share and target-band economics.

market_boundary.dominant_subsegment: Mixed analytical supply-chain improvement with material implementation and change-management work.
market_boundary.selection_basis: The code excludes freight brokers and 3PL operations but overlaps general management consulting and back-office freight audit. The central case therefore reflects an in-band specialist consultancy rather than a pure broker, software vendor or clerical audit processor.

top_uncertain_inputs.score_impact: A faster or slower conversion of client AI use into consultant workflows materially changes timing.
Capture changes sharply with the unknown gainshare/value versus effort-priced revenue mix.
The multiple uplift disappears if specialization, founder independence and repeatability are not demonstrated.
A field-implementation-heavy six-digit workforce would reduce available labor savings relative to the four-digit proxy.

reviewer_note: Underwrite revenue by engagement type before applying this central case. A gainshare freight-cost specialist with proprietary data and implementation capability is economically different from a project-based analyst shop selling network studies.
